title: After-action review in 20 minutes
summary: Ask what was expected, what happened, what helped, what failed, what nearly failed, and one change for the next operation.
content: Ask what was expected, what happened, what helped, what failed, what nearly failed, and one change for the next operation. Assign each correction an owner and date before the group disperses. Do not turn the review into blame, punishment, or removal of inconvenient facts.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “After-action review in 20 minutes”.
Relevant labels, contacts, and official alerts — for «After-action review in 20 minutes»
steps: Ask what was expected, what happened, what helped, what failed, what nearly failed, and one change for the next operation.
Assign each correction an owner and date before the group disperses.
Assign one person to act and another to verify the result for After-action review in 20 minutes.
Record time, source, action, result, unresolved risk, and next review for After-action review in 20 minutes.
warnings: Do not turn the review into blame, punishment, or removal of inconvenient facts.
Official instructions and trained responders override this offline card for After-action review in 20 minutes.
common_mistakes: Acting before location, time, hazard, and responsible person are clear for After-action review in 20 minutes.
Failing to update the plan when conditions change during After-action review in 20 minutes.
alternatives: Use a paper note, SMS, radio message, or trusted messenger when the main system is unavailable for After-action review in 20 minutes.
failure_signs: The decision source, responsible person, or next review cannot be identified for After-action review in 20 minutes.
The action creates a new hazard or delays qualified help during After-action review in 20 minutes.
when_not_to_use: Do not use this card to override an evacuation order, emergency responder, clinician, product label, or local law for After-action review in 20 minutes.
short_term: For After-action review in 20 minutes, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After After-action review in 20 minutes, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop After-action review in 20 minutes, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
environment_notes: Adapt After-action review in 20 minutes to Greek heat, wildfire smoke, earthquakes, islands, mountain roads, apartment stairs, power cuts, water interruptions, mobility limits, and official alerts.
sources: CDC — Water, sanitation and hygiene emergencies: https://www.cdc.gov/water-emergency/index.html
CDC — Keep food safe after an emergency: https://www.cdc.gov/food-safety/foods/keep-food-safe-after-emergency.html
